sourceMovie.title: Contagion
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Contagion works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: I Am Legend
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: I Am Legend (2007) is the kind of film Contagion fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The drama overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Rise of the Planet of the Apes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Rise of the Planet of the Apes shares Contagion's gift for drama — the 2011 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Outbreak
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Contagion compelling — the drama, the thriller — shows up in Outbreak (1995) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Blindness
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama energy of Contagion is alive in Blindness (2008), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Omega Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Omega Man (1971) operates in the same drama as Contagion, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Terminator (1984) belongs to the same conversation as Contagion — both anchor their thriller in science-fiction emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Contagion's thriller stayed with you, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The science-fiction through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Twelve Monkeys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Contagion excels in thriller, Twelve Monkeys (1995) doubles down on science-fiction to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Day After Tomorrow
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Day After Tomorrow (2004) is the natural next step after Contagion: same thriller DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and science-fiction elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Armageddon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Contagion who respond to its thriller will find Armageddon (1998) immediately familiar. Both films share a science-fiction sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

faq.question: What should I watch after Contagion?
faq.answer: Try I Am Legend, Rise of the Planet of the Apes, Outbreak—each shares DNA with Contagion while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Contagion?
faq.answer: Contagion (2011) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Contagion worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
